description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-002-server.md:65
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: FEAT-002 now introduces a canonical multi-project contract in the new Project Registry and Routing section (`GET /api/projects`, `GET /api/projects/:project`, explicit selection rules, and unscoped routes as compatibility aliases only), but the numbered functional requirements still define only legacy unscoped `/api/...` endpoints and unqualified MCP tool names for documents, beads, doc graph, agent sessions, executions, bead mutations, and document writes. Review issue

acceptance: ddx bead update <id> --set closing_commit_sha=not-a-sha fails with a validation error; ddx bead update <id> --set closing_commit_sha=<short-valid-sha> stores the full commit SHA; and regression tests cover both the reject-invalid and normalize-valid repair paths
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:403
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit c38d7bbb2cf47baed8197c27cac19cb4adcd8644 documents ddx bead update <id> --set closing_commit_sha=<sha> as the supported provenance-repair path and adds a happy-path test for closed beads, but the underlying bead update implementation still routes closing_commit_sha through the generic --set handler without any normalization or verification. Unlike bead close --commit, which resolves the value through git rev-parse --verify <sha>^{commit}, bead update accepts arbitrary strings such as not-a-sha and persists them to closed beads. That leaves FEAT-019 replay and audit provenance one bad repair away from silently storing an invalid or ambiguous commit boundary even though the docs now present this as the safe replacement for hand-editing storage.
Suggested fix: Teach ddx bead update --set closing_commit_sha=... to use the same commit-resolution path as bead close --commit and reject invalid or non-commit values, then add a regression test proving invalid repair SHAs fail and valid abbreviated SHAs are normalized to full commits.
title: correctness: validate closing_commit_sha repairs before persisting

acceptance: ddx bead show hx-7c184555 --json omits closing_commit_sha, and ddx bead show hx-6855d237 --json no longer reports closing_commit_sha=89d7b1193c0d8c8f980e955576da6a7545f36b04 while remaining open until that repair lands or closing only after its acceptance is satisfied
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:86
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit f58e708dc6672763e7adb31e294fc26d4da1b7db appends hx-6855d237 as a closed review-finding bead, but its acceptance requires ddx bead show hx-7c184555 --json to omit closing_commit_sha and that condition is still false after the commit. The reviewed diff only adds the hx-6855d237 row; it does not repair hx-7c184555, and ddx bead show hx-7c184555 --json still reports closing_commit_sha=2361670c68103160987c9851851819317a3b357e. The newly added row also records closing_commit_sha=89d7b1193c0d8c8f980e955576da6a7545f36b04, so replay/audit now shows hx-6855d237 as complete even though the governed repair never landed.
Suggested fix: Reopen or otherwise repair hx-6855d237 through supported ddx bead commands, clear hx-7c184555.closing_commit_sha as its acceptance requires, and only close hx-6855d237 after the tracker state actually satisfies that acceptance with non-misleading provenance.
title: correctness: do not close hx-6855d237 before its acceptance is true

acceptance: ddx bead show hx-7599316a --json reports closing_commit_sha=a6f7e9d3c43e1fc0705ea8e3077a4e4b25d3a58c, and git show --name-only --format=oneline a6f7e9d3c43e1fc0705ea8e3077a4e4b25d3a58c includes docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md and .ddx/beads.jsonl
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:204
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit a6f7e9d3c43e1fc0705ea8e3077a4e4b25d3a58c correctly updates TP-002 to mark TC-010 as planned future coverage, but the same commit adds bead hx-7599316a with closing_commit_sha=040008c8471a99885313e5c0e7cb387de7c70196. That SHA is the prior HEAD and does not include the TP-002 fix that actually resolved the finding. FEAT-019 replay and audit flows use closing_commit_sha as the implementation boundary, so this review-finding bead now points at unrelated provenance even though the durable tracker row claims the finding is closed.
Suggested fix: Repair hx-7599316a so closing_commit_sha points at a6f7e9d3c43e1fc0705ea8e3077a4e4b25d3a58c, and keep the close/repair workflow aligned so beads closed by a landing commit record that landing commit instead of the previous HEAD.
title: correctness: repair hx-7599316a closing provenance to the actual fix commit

acceptance: Registry.Discover() uses the same LookPath abstraction as Runner.resolveHarness; a test with injected LookPath controls both routing and discovery results
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: agent harness discovery. Evidence covers Registry.Discover LookPath injection alignment with Runner.
</context-digest>
Registry.Discover() calls exec.LookPath directly instead of using the Runner's injected LookPath function.

## Impact
Tests that inject a custom LookPath cannot fully control Discover() behavior. Any future LookPath customization (caching, PATH filtering) won't apply to discovery.

## Fix required
Add a LookPath field to Registry (or pass it via constructor) so Discover() uses the same abstraction as Runner.
title: Wire LookPath injection through Registry.Discover
